products.description:
ETHERFAX GovCloud is a secure, scalable, and compliant cloud-based fax solution designed to modernize enterprise faxing while offering the flexibility to leverage existing investments. Built with a security-first posture, our US government community cloud-based fax solution has been engineered to meet the federal government?s rigorous FedRAMP HIGH security standards. Hosted in AWS' FedRAMP HIGH GovCloud environment that utilizes FIPS-140-3 certified cryptographic algorithms, our data exchange solutions ensure the highest levels of data protection and encryption for government workflows. Leveraging the power of AWS GovCloud and supported by a dedicated US-based team, GovCloud delivers high availability, resiliency, and seamless integrations for government and regulated industries.

Key Features:

Integrations
?        GovCloud Microsoft Teams: Seamlessly integrates with Microsoft Teams in GovCloud for streamlined communication workflows.
?        GovCloud Office 365: Supports Office 365 in GovCloud environments, enabling secure collaboration and faxing.
?        Google Workspace: Integrates with Google Workspace for enhanced productivity and interoperability.
?        GovCloud API: Provides a robust API for custom integrations, allowing organizations to tailor ETHERFAX to their specific needs.

High Availability and Resiliency
?        AWS GovCloud East and West: Deployed across AWS GovCloud (US-East and US-West) regions, AWS GovCloud ensures high availability and resiliency through geographically distributed data centers, supporting mission-critical workloads.
?        Elastic Cloud Computing: Dynamically scale resources to meet demand, delivering consistent performance during peak usage.

Scalability and Data Security
?        Containerization: Utilizes containerized architecture to enable on-demand scaling, providing flexibility and efficiency for fluctuating workloads.
?        Segregated S3 Buckets: Employs isolated Amazon S3 buckets to segregate data, ensuring data separation and enhancing security and compliance.

Compliance
?        FedRAMP High IL5: Meets stringent FedRAMP High and DoD Impact Level 5 (IL5) requirements, suitable for sensitive and regulated workloads.
?        Section 508: Ensures accessibility compliance for users with disabilities, adhering to federal standards.
?        HITRUST and PCI Level 4 Certified: Certified to meet rigorous healthcare and payment card industry security standards, ensuring robust data protection.

Seamless Integration with Existing Systems 
?        RightFax or Biscom: Transition to a more secure cloud platform with the option to integrate with RightFax or Biscom, enabling continued use of your current fax infrastructure without a costly rip-and-replace approach.
?        Leverage Existing Investments: Choose to maintain your existing workflows and configurations while upgrading to a modern, cloud-based solution.

Dedicated Support
?        US-Based Support Team: ETHERFAX offers a dedicated, US-based support team available to assist with implementation, troubleshooting, and ongoing maintenance, ensuring reliable service for all customers.

ETHERFAX GovCloud combines the flexibility of cloud technology with the security and compliance required for government and regulated industries. By leveraging AWS GovCloud?s isolated infrastructure, containerized scalability, and segregated data storage, ETHERFAX provides a future-proof fax solution with the option to integrate seamlessly with existing systems and modern collaboration tools. With a commitment to compliance and a US-based support team, ETHERFAX GovCloud empowers organizations to modernize faxing while maintaining security, reliability, and operational efficiency.

Checkmarx One for Government (CXG) is a cutting-edge, cloud-native application security platform designed specifically for U.S. government agencies. Hosted in AWS GovCloud, CXG provides a secure, single-tenant environment that supports the most stringent compliance standards, enabling government entities to safely develop, deploy, and monitor applications in a highly secure manner.

CXG offers a comprehensive suite of security modules, including Static Application Security Testing (SAST), Software Composition Analysis (SCA), and Application Security Posture Management (ASPM). Additionally, CXG provides advanced add-ons for Malicious Package Detection, Container Security, and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) Security, all of which enable government agencies to secure applications from code to cloud and across the entire software development lifecycle (SDLC).

Seamlessly integrating into developer workflows through IDE plugins (such as VSCode), CLI, API, and a web-based UI, CXG empowers developers to identify and fix vulnerabilities early in the SDLC. The platform provides actionable remediation guidance and risk-based prioritization, ensuring that vulnerabilities are addressed before they can impact mission-critical systems.

CXG continuously scans proprietary code, open-source dependencies, containers, and IaC templates using Checkmarx Threat Intelligence to proactively detect exploitable risks. Its developer-first experience allows secure software development without disruption, and its compliance features ensure alignment with industry standard benchmarks for security and risk management. Hosted in AWS GovCloud and built to meet the highest U.S. government security standards, CXG automates security across DevSecOps pipelines.

CXG also ensures continuous monitoring of security controls, providing real-time insights into the security posture of your applications and infrastructure. It is purpose-built to meet the unique security and compliance needs of U.S. government agencies, enabling a secure and resilient software development process in today?s complex cybersecurity landscape.

The platform?s Data Intelligence Engine understands the unique semantics and usage patterns of an organization?s data, offering natural language access, tailored recommendations, and robust governance and privacy controls.

Guard provides enterprises with an automated, proactive approach to Salesforce security. With AutoRABIT Guard, enterprises can transform their Salesforce environments from a security liability into a well-governed, compliant, and resilient system without slowing down innovation or burdening teams with manual processes. products.description:
IBM Data Services for Government is delivered as SaaS offering using a multi-tenant cloud computing environment. IBM Data Services for Government is a portfolio of AI products that accelerates the impact of generative AI in core workflows to drive productivity.

IBM watsonx.ai for Government is an integrated AI development studio that makes building and deploying AI simple and scalable. It includes tools, foundation models, and flexible runtimes ready for use as-a-Service. IBM watsonx.ai provides a suite of developer-focused capabilities such as SDKs, APIs, retrieval augmented generation (RAG) frameworks and templates, and more to streamline AI application development. Clients can access business-ready foundation models ranging from IBM Granite, open source models and models from partners such as Meta, to build AI applications for any agency use case.

IBM watsonx.data for Government is a data management suite built for AI providing services to improve gen AI accuracy and performance using unified structured and unstructured data with a no-code, low-code and pro-code user experiences that foster self-service access to data insights. IBM watsonx.data has a unique multi-engine architecture optimized for price and performance. Clients can automate the ingestion, enrichment, and delivery of unstructured data at scale and unify it with structured data. The result? Context-aware and accurate AI that fuels confident decision-making.

IBM watsonx.governance for Government is an end-to-end AI governance service that seamlessly integrates with your existing systems to automate and accelerate responsible AI workflows to help save time, reduce costs and comply with regulations. Clients can direct, manage, and monitor their AI using a single service to speed up responsible, transparent, and explainable AI. IBM watsonx.governance automates and scales end-to-end AI governance enabling clients to seamlessly develop, deploy, monitor and replace models across any IBM or third-party services such as OpenAI and Amazon SageMaker. Take advantage of automated tools that provide risk metrics to stay ahead of threats and simplify compliance with services that automate the process of identifying regulatory changes and translating them into enforceable policies.

IBM watsonx Orchestrate for Government is a single solution to build, test, and deploy AI agents, assistants that automate workflows and processes with generative AI. Clients can browse custom-built agents, IBM watsonx agents and trusted partner solutions. Search by domain, use case or application name to quickly find the right agent for the job. IBM watsonx Orchestrate seamlessly integrates with existing business systems, connects to any AI model or automation tool, and enables collaboration between AI assistants and agents in one unified experience. The outcome? Less manual work. Faster decisions. A more efficient business, at scale. IBM watsonx Orchestate enables AI agents to plan, coordinate and take action together. Whether it?s one agent handling a task or a team collaborating across tools, watsonx Orchestrate intelligently assigns the right agents and resources to get work done, without human micromanagement.

IBM watsonx.data intelligence for Government transforms raw data into actionable insights swiftly, unify data governance, quality and sharing, and empowers data consumers with reliable and contextualized data. IBM watsonx.data intelligence accelerates data discovery, resolves quality issues and automates data governance. Clients can discover and assess data quality at scale?wherever your data resides?with automated capabilities for data profiling, cleansing, monitoring and more. Ensure accuracy, trust and compliance by mapping the journey of your data. Break organizational siloes and enable faster data sharing by automating the delivery of data products.

IBM watsonx.data integration for Government service provides a unified control plane for modern data movement, helping government agencies connect, transform, and deliver trusted data wherever it resides. Start with ETL and ELT pipelines for batch integration, then expand as needs evolve to include streaming, replication, and unstructured data, all supported by built-in observability. Deploy and run pipelines across cloud, on-premises, or hybrid environments while maintaining consistent governance and control. By turning raw data into AI-ready data, watsonx.data integration unifies sources, fuels model training, and enhances AI?s contextual understanding. It selects the optimal integration style to meet goals for performance, cost, latency, and security, ensuring fast and reliable access to trusted data across the entire data fabric.

The Mimic platform delivers a high-assurance ransomware resilience layer that operates at runtime to stop damage and maintain operational continuity. Built for speed, deception, and recovery, Mimic protects critical applications before ransomware can encrypt or exfiltrate data. Unlike traditional tools, Mimic deploys in the kernel, enabling rapid deflection, application immutability, and lifeboat-based recovery. The platform includes the following core components:

Automated Deflection and Runtime Resilience
Early and Deterministic Response: Mimic continuously monitors application behavior at the kernel level across Windows and Linux systems. At the first sign of ransomware or unauthorized change, protection is triggered in real time?without reliance on manual SOC involvement.

Automated Deflection Measures: Mimic acts faster than ransomware can execute, deploying the following capabilities automatically:
Application Immutability: Critical applications are instantly locked into a protected state, preventing any changes from being made. This protects both against malicious encryption and violations of internal change policies.
AI-Driven Deception: When an attack is detected, Mimic fabricates decoy data that mirrors real applications and critical assets. These AI-generated lures convince attackers they have succeeded?slowing them down and buying time for response and forensics.
Adversary Containment: By operating inside the application?s runtime environment, Mimic halts ransomware progression deterministically, even in cases of zero-days or evasive techniques.

Recovery Through the Lifeboat Approach
Isolated Recovery in Minutes: Mimic?s recovery workflows leverage the Lifeboat?an isolated, clean environment where compromised applications are relaunched safely. This ensures continued operation while the primary environment is investigated and remediated.
Resilient Recovery Process: The platform enables a secure and controlled recovery that includes:
Spinning up trusted application instances in a separate environment,
Enforcing authentication controls to prevent attacker reentry, and
Reintegrating recovered systems in a compliant, structured manner.

Testing and Assurance Tools
Continuous Validation with Customers: Mimic supports ongoing evaluation of its controls in collaboration with the customer?s security team through built-in testing features.
Arena: A secure sandbox that mirrors production environments, enabling customers to simulate attacks and observe platform behavior in controlled conditions.
Signal Generator: A ransomware simulation engine that replicates real-world adversary tactics?allowing validation of deflection and recovery mechanisms without risking operational harm.

By integrating ultra-fast response, deception-based defense, and secure recovery, the Mimic platform delivers a last line of defense against ransomware. These automated controls are designed to support internal security frameworks and compliance with standards such as GDPR, SOC 2, FedRAMP, and IL 4?ensuring that even when ransomware breaches initial defenses, operations continue without compromise.

Archive360 provides a modern archive and governance platform to address the challenge of a growing number of digital communications channels, legal and regulatory record retention, data privacy and security requirements. Compliantly collecting, retaining, and managing growing volumes of data from multiple channels in a timely manner is increasingly important to department and agency missions, and difficult without the proper tools. Archive360?s platform helps manage data consistent with digital record keeping requirements, such as the Federal Records Act, NARA Records Management Rules, and OMB directives on transition to electronic records.  
Archive360?s solutions enable adoption of the federal government?s Cloud First mandates, Executive Orders as to cloud optimization, and OMB?s directives including M-23-07.  Archive360?s solutions have been successfully deployed and trusted by public sector agencies globally in the U.S., U.K., Canada, and Australia, including numerous components of U.S. security, civilian intelligence, educational, veterans? agencies as well as individual US States.
Archive360?s digital communications governance and archiving solution brings together data from common channels and legacy archives into a petabyte-scale platform that: 
?        Ensures that digital communications are captured, governed, and readily accessible to authorized stakeholders
?        Provides extensive retention and disposition capabilities supporting government and agency records management requirements
?        Enables data classification across communication and data channels for granular governance controls
?        Supports file and data archiving use cases
?        Provides secure, scalable eDiscovery and FOIA-search capabilities through an intuitive user interface
?        Provides case management, tagging and annotation capabilities for investigations and review and production of data 
?        Delivers cross-system visibility and control over data processing, retention, disposition and legal holds 
?        Ensures data authenticity and chain of custody with a complete audit trail for actions (system and user) within the platform 
?        Cloud-native platform that provides high-levels of data durability, resiliency, and immutability where required
?        Zero trust security model, which requires specific entitlement grants to access any data. The platform incorporates an entitlement engine, which allows for granular access controls, including restrictions based on role, class of data, functionality
?        Encryption of all data in motion and at rest, with the option for agency managed encryption keys

Security and scalability are paramount for government departments and agencies to achieve their data governance mission.  Archive360 deploys each agency in a dedicated SaaS environment, deployed to their own tenant. As a result, each department, agency or organizational unit has completely segregated data, administrative access, entitlements, and the ability to integrate into their security protocols. 

Archive360 is deployed as a cloud-native, microservices architecture. Archive360 can tailor the deployment to specific mission workloads and requirements. This deployment model mitigates data migration or exit risk at the end of a term, since the tenant can be turned over to the department or agency with the resident data.

Throughout the platform, Archive360 maintains an extensive event audit history, supporting a complete chain of custody. This includes data processing, access, changes to configurations and controls, search, disposition, or export, all at an object level. This provides rapid traceability for audit and response purposes, and defensibility of authorized activities. 
Supported digital communication channels include:
?        SMTP and Journal Based Email
?        Microsoft M365 and Exchange
?        Microsoft Teams
?        Google Workspace
?        SMS and Mobile Data
?        FileShare Data
?        Legacy Email Archives

products.cso: ThreatLocker Zero Trust Platform for Government
products.description:
ThreatLocker endpoint protection platform secures customer endpoints (laptops/desktops/servers) running Windows and MacOS using a policy driven deny-by-default approach. 
ThreatLocker's Allowlisting permits software, applications, and scripts to run only if they have been explicitly approved to run and blocks all others from running by default. 
ThreatLocker's Ringfencing enables organizations to control what applications are permitted to do whilst running, controlling, and limiting, applications' access to other applications, files, registry, and the internet. This stops legitimate and trusted applications being weaponized. 
ThreatLocker's Network Control is an endpoint firewall permitting only required and explicitly permitted network connectivity and connections and blocking all others by default. Network Control is also used to only allow specified protected devices to communicate with each other, by opening and closing ports dynamically. 
ThreatLocker's Storage Control offers granular control over both storage devices, such as USB's, and also locations. Data can be protected from exfiltration and damage by limiting which specific applications have access to it. 
ThreatLocker's Configuration Manager allows organizations to set configuration policies such as password complexity requirements disabling unneeded network protocols across an organization or set of organizations from one central location without the need to use group policies or active directory. 
ThreatLocker Detect uses the voluminous data in ThreatLocker logs and allows organizations to both alert themselves and respond automatically to anomalous events or suspicious behavior on their endpoints. In summary, the ThreatLocker zero-trust endpoint protection platform allows organizations to: 
? Block ransomware, viruses, malware, and rogue software from running. 
? Stop users from running unknown or unapproved software. 
? Stop lateral movement between applications and systems. 
? Stop trusted applications from being weaponized. 
? Control access to storage devices. 
? Audit and control data access by applications. 
? Quickly deploy and run an audit for the entire organization. 
? Update permissions quickly when requested and approved by IT. 
? Log and control all inbound network traffic. 
? Alert on and respond to defined anomalous events.

The Anomali Cloud Service Offering consists of two integrated components: Security Analytics and ThreatStream, delivered as a unified cloud-native security platform. Security Analytics provides AI and cloud-native security information and event management (SIEM) capabilities including data collection and normalization from network devices, endpoints, applications, and cloud services; real-time event correlation and threat detection using machine learning algorithms; behavioral analytics and anomaly detection; incident response workflows; and query capabilities across petabytes of historical data stored in an integrated security data lake with configurable retention periods up to 7+ years. The platform supports ingestion of 30TB+ of data within hours and enables sub-second query response times across petabyte-scale datasets using natural language processing and AI-supported interfaces.

ThreatStream provides threat intelligence management capabilities including automated collection, normalization, and curation of threat intelligence from commercial feeds, open source intelligence, and information sharing centers; management of indicators of compromise (IOCs) and indicators of attack (IOAs) with automated deduplication and confidence scoring; correlation of external threat intelligence with internal security telemetry; distribution of actionable intelligence to integrated security tools via APIs and standard protocols; threat analysis and enrichment with MITRE ATT&CK framework mapping; and secure community-based threat sharing. The service operates as a multi-tenant Software-as-a-Service solution with role-based access controls, data encryption in transit and at rest, comprehensive audit logging, and RESTful APIs for integration with existing security infrastructure. Service boundaries include all data ingestion interfaces, processing engines, storage systems, user interfaces, APIs, and integration points necessary to deliver security analytics and threat intelligence management capabilities.

Combined these enable Footprint to achieve no system downtime for upgrades and maintenance.

Secure: Compliant with NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 Control Baseline-Moderate, PCI DSS v4.0.1, FISMA-Moderate, fully implements FedRAMP Moderate, and uses strict security processes for access to protected data (e.g., Personally Identifiable Information (PII)).

Business Driven: Footprint reduces time to market bringing core business functions such as account management, internal business management tools, multi-channel user communications and customer engagement, secure payment processing, financial transaction management, content management, cart and checkout, integratedanalytics, and real-time reporting and dashboards, and customer service management. Mobile Application: Customer and management services are also delivered through fully mobile responsive user experience and native mobile applications for iOS and Android. The mobile app includes mobile-specific functions like ApplePay, Google Pay, QR code scanning, and the use of geolocation.

Confluent Cloud for Government (CCG) Software-as-a-Service
(SaaS) is a fully managed, cloud-native Kafka service for connecting
and processing data using a cloud computing environment. It enables
its customers to focus on business needs instead of managing the
underlying infrastructure supporting their Kafka instances(s). It is
available to public, federal, state, local and tribal governments, as well
as research institutions, federal contractors, government contractors,
etc.

The CCG offering is comprised of two components ? the Cloud
Control Plane and Data Plane (Kafka clusters). The Cloud Control
Plane is hosted in Amazon Web Services (AWS) GovCloud and
Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and is used by customers to select and
deploy their managed Kafka resources to a Satellite Cluster. The
Satellite Cluster consists of one or many Kubernetes nodes running
Kafka, with the one or many availability zones (AZs) deployed based
on options selected by the customer. A customer can choose to
deploy a Satellite Cluster to AWS GovCloud. The Control Plane is
used to manage resources in Satellite Clusters using the Control
Pipeline. All customer data is retained in Satellite Clusters and no
customer data from the Satellite Clusters is sent to the Control Plane.

Core Functions and Components:

Only components deployed within this AWS GovCloud boundary and specified within the system?s SSP are included in the authorization. All services are monitored, maintained, and operated by Lifebit staff cleared to work with government systems, and all infrastructure complies with FedRAMP requirements.

products.description:
DSS Health Cloud is delivered as a PaaS/SaaS offering using a multitenant Government-Only Cloud computing environment. It is available to VA Hospital system(s).  As a cloud-based solution hosted in AWS GovCloud, the DSS Health Cloud provides to VA Clinicians, Administrators, Managers, and Users the TeleCare Record Management Plus-Clinical Triage (TRMW), Patient Case Manager High Reliability Organization (PCM HRO/VSPM), RxTracker (RTVX), and Juno EHR (JEHR) applications for the purpose of conducting clinical and administrative activities in a reliable and high-security federal cloud environment.

SaaS
The following SaaS applications are provided within the DSS Health Cloud solution.

TeleCare Record Manager Plus-Clinical Triage (TRMW) is a web based application that provides VHA healthcare facilities with an intuitive, user-friendly interface for telecare triage providers. TRM Plus-CT creates progress notes which are then seamlessly incorporated into the patient?s existing EHR.
?        Centralized web-based interface which allows users to access all VistA facilities from a single interface.
?        Enterprise-wide ad hoc analytics that delivers reporting at the local, VISN, or enterprise level.
?        Seamlessly integrated with VistA Scheduling Enhancements (VSE) to allow creation, update, and cancelation of appointments.
?        Is VA Technical Reference Model compliant.
Patient Case Manager High Reliability Organization (PCM HRO) standardizes workflows and combines data visualization tools and analytics to reduce Veteran suicide prevention care variation and impact Mental Health SAIL metrics.
?        Actively monitors suicide prevention processes, performance, and outcomes.
?        Automates data capture and reporting to prevent lapses in care.
?        Improves transparency in the management of high-risk patients across the enterprise
?        Automates identification and escalation of scenarios requiring intervention.
?        Automates case load reporting and changes over time.
With PCM, hospitals leverage real-time data and analytics for greater oversight and more effective, standardized workflows. Providers will save time, reduce medical errors, and ensure every patient receives the care and satisfaction they deserve.
?        Improve Patient Safety
?        ? Monitor Quality and Performance
?        ? Maximize Patient Throughput with the Patient Flow Module

RxTracker is a single, comprehensive ePrescribing solution that gives providers the ability to effectively perform their full clinical workflow for all care settings: inpatient, outpatient, and ambulatory.
1.        Proactive and Reactive Patient Safety Alerts
2.        EPCS
3.        Metrics, Reporting, and Auditing

Juno EHR is a cloud-based electronic health record suite of applications that automates administrative and clinical workflows within a healthcare organization. The solution provides features to manage all aspects of patient care such as scheduling, registration, clinical notes, order entry, and electronic prescription.
All DSS Health Cloud Software applications, with their corresponding components, are hosted within the SaaS authorization boundary. Applications are VA VistA integrated and follow a role-based design model. Using the VA provided and managed PIV smart card, users are authenticated through the VA Identity Authentication Management System (IAM). During the login authentication process, users with active and valid credentials are logged into the application following all FedRAMP High, VA, and organization protocols. All web transactions and data transmissions between the applications and the integrated VistA database go through a VPC/Palo Alto Firewall into the VA Network. The VPC/Palo Alto firewall controls access and monitors LS 1.2 CI/CD pipelines, external services, communication with the VA Network, and with the internet.
Software application input activities are recorded into audit logs, which meet the FedRAMP high-security level as well as DSS organizational auditing security standards. All audit logs are collected by the Splunk SIEM tool, which triggers near-real-time alerts and notifications to DSS Health Cloud Engineers for analysis and continuous monitoring. Logs are stored within the FedRAMP authorization boundary into S3 buckets for seven years for after-the fact investigations of security incidents and to meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

PaaS
DSS Health Cloud offers the VA hospital Systems a robust and secure multi-tenant PaaS platform that allows flexible integration with other VA vendors' software applications.

OpenAI's ChatGPT services allow users to interact with OpenAI's models through a conversational interface.  Users can request the models to analyze or generate text. Wiz for U.S. Government

Wiz for U.S. Government (?Wiz for Gov?) is a cloud-native environment and product line that delivers a unified view of risk across a customer?s full cloud environment, from code to production. Wiz for Gov helps public sector agencies meet FedRAMP and FISMA compliance needs by focusing on the issues that matter most. The Wiz for Gov unified product line has three different components to secure your stack:


Wiz Cloud for Gov

Wiz Cloud focuses on assessing and prioritizing risk by analyzing vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, malware, identities, network paths, data, and AI risks. At its core is the Wiz Security Graph, which maps relationships between cloud resources to find "toxic combinations" that represent real risk. This approach automates risk assessments in alignment with OMB A-130's definition of risk as a function of the magnitude of harm and the likelihood of occurrence. It prioritizes these risks and routes them to relevant teams, providing a clear view of the attack path, blast radius, and recommended remediation steps.


Wiz Code for Gov

Wiz Code extends visibility into the development lifecycle to help enforce secure-by-design practices. By connecting directly to code repositories, Wiz provides full traceability from production issues back to the corresponding source code and owner. Wiz also integrates with CI/CD pipelines to enforce policies during image scanning and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) reviews, helping to identify risks earlier and accelerate time to production readiness. This shared view helps security and development teams collaborate, ensuring accountability and reducing mean time to remediation (MTTR).


Wiz Defend for Gov

Wiz Defend modernizes cloud threat detection and response by reducing alert fatigue and surfacing the root cause of issues faster. It leverages a cloud native runtime sensor, logs, and threat intelligence to identify malicious activity and enrich detected events with cloud context, providing SOC analysts with the information needed to assess threat impact and blast radius. This enhances active cyber defense across the full environment?including identity, workload runtime, network, data, and control plane components?and helps teams align response actions with the MITRE ATT&CK framework.

Vinesign is the electronic signature component of the Filevine suite. It enables government legal teams, contract and compliance departments, and related legal services to prepare, send, and execute documents electronically in compliance with legal and security standards. Vinesign provides an auditable record of document execution, supports records governance requirements, and integrates with Filevine case and contract management tools to maintain continuity of documentation workflows.

Versa Unified SASE for Government includes the following capabilities:
- Versa Secure SD-WAN
- Versa Secure Private Access / Zero-Trust Network Access (ZTNA) / Security Services Edge (SSE)
- Versa Secure Internet Access / Next-Gen Firewall as a Service (NG-FWaaS), Next-Gen Secure Web Gateway (NG-SWG)
- Versa Secure Fabric / Secure Private & Internet Access (combines ZTNA and NG-SWG)
- Inline Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)
- Network Data Loss Prevention (DLP)
- Digital Experience Monitoring (DEM)
- Hybrid management & orchestration of both cloud-delivered and on-premise Versa Operating System (VOS) gateways
- Shared or dedicated private instances

Versa Unified SASE for Government is hosted within the AWS GovCloud environment. To support high availability, geographic redundancy, and secure isolation, the architecture leverages multiple availability zones (AZs) and virtual private clouds (VPCs). These configurations ensure separation between customer environments and the Versa Networks, Inc. (Versa)'s management infrastructure.

The Versa management infrastructure is hosted on Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instances with all associated components, including customer VPCs and instances, which are regularly backed up in AWS. This ensures continuity of operations and recoverability in the event of a disruption.

Services within the system boundary are organized into three logical tiers: Access Tier 1 (SMX Elevate Intelligent Automation Platform (IAP), Tier 2 (Versa), and Tier 3 (Customer). Each tier is logically isolated using the Amazon VPC service to maintain strong separation of duties, security boundaries, and operational control.

The tenant leverages the security and General Support System (GSS) services of The Armory | FedRAMP Marketplace to provide a known-secure deployment location, leveraging a majority of GCP FedRAMP authorized native cloud and in-boundary security services, for CSPs seeking authorization under 20x.

As part of the FedRAMP 20x Pilot, CSPs must report their Key Security Indicator (KSI) status as defined by the FedRAMP 20x Key Security Indicators | FedRAMP.gov. Armory20x leverages stackArmor's automated assessment solution to provide status for all required KSIs for a CSP deployed within a 20x tenant.
Tenants are heavily customizable, allowing Armory20x customers to deploy workloads that are suited to their particular application and service needs.
stackArmor personnel assist CSPs/ISVs with deploying services in a manner that meets FedRAMP 20x KSI requirements and helps ensure an authorizable status under the 20x program.

products.description:
Vantage for Government is a cloud-native SaaS platform specifically designed to meet the security needs of federal agencies and critical infrastructure operators. It delivers asset visibility, threat detection, and response across IT, OT, and IoT environments.

Visibility
- Asset Inventory ? the platform combines passive, active, and host-based data collection methods with AI-driven analysis and enrichment. This ensures visibility across wired, wireless, and endpoint environments. Add-ons such as Asset Intelligence and Threat Intelligence enrich device profiles with lifecycle data and vulnerabilities. Smart Polling complements discovery methods for more accurate profiling. Integrations with third-party applications and project file import capabilities further enrich asset profiles.  

- Vulnerability and Exposure Management ? The platform provides non-intrusive OT/IoT vulnerability detection with prioritized remediation recommendations

- Risk Management ? The platform supports the full risk management lifecycle:  
- Risk Identification through asset inventory, network mapping, vulnerability detection, and threat monitoring.  
- Risk Assessment with customizable, multifactor scoring at the asset, zone, site, and enterprise level.  
- Risk Mitigation through prioritized remediation recommendations based on impact and criticality.  
- Risk Monitoring with executive dashboards with real-time risk trends, site-level accountability, and industry benchmarks.  

Detection

- Threat Detection ? The platform monitors wired, wireless, and endpoint activity. It detects cyber threats using built-in rules and continuously updated OT/IoT threat intelligence. Vantage IQ add-on correlates and prioritizes alerts to reduce false positives. 

- Process Anomaly Detection ? The platform extracts commands and process telemetry through deep packet inspection of OT/IoT protocols and via Arc Embedded sensors for RTUs and PLCs, and detect process telemetry, control command, and device behavior anomalies.  

Response

- Integrations ? The platform connects with SIEM, SOAR, firewalls, NAC, ticketing, and other SOC tools, for automated incident response.  

- Customizable Playbooks ? User-defined playbooks standardize manual incident response. 
 
- AI-Driven Remediation Recommendations ? The AI engine correlates attack and anomaly telemetry and focus on actions with the highest risk-reduction impact.

- Endpoint Response ? The Arc sensor extends incident response to endpoints, supporting user-defined prevention modes including detection-only, quarantine, and automatic removal.

products.3pao_name: A-LIGN Compliance and Security, Inc. dba A-LIGN
products.description:
Keeper Security Government Cloud (KSGC) is a SaaS-based password management and privileged access management (PAM) platform that enables government agencies to secure, manage and monitor access to critical infrastructure and endpoints. Every user and device in the organization is protected with a zero-trust security model with authentication, authorization and encryption.

The PAM platform unifies essential cybersecurity products into one ubiquitous platform which covers (i) password and credential management (ii) secrets management (iii) multi-protocol connection management (SSH, RDP, VNC, etc.) which supports zero-trust network access with connections, tunneling and remote browser isolation and (iv) endpoint privilege management for enforce zero-standing privilege and just-in-time access.

The system can automatically rotate credentials and secrets on demand or as scheduled to prevent credential theft. Privileged sessions can be monitored and recorded, down to the keystroke, for enforcing complete audit trails and compliance. Asset discovery provides centralized visibility into all privileged accounts, machines and databases across cloud and on-prem infrastructure.

KSGC is built on a Zero-Knowledge Encryption (ZKE) architecture. All encryption and decryption occurs locally on the user?s device, and encryption keys never leave the client. KSGC cannot access, view, or decrypt customer data, including passwords, records, files, or master passwords.

User data is encrypted using AES-256 (AES-GCM) and remains encrypted during storage, transmission, and synchronization through the KSGC Cloud Security Vault. Encrypted data may be synchronized across authorized user devices, but only the end user can decrypt it after successful authentication.

Users authenticate via organization-defined methods, including Single Sign-On (SSO) or Keeper native authentication, with support for multi-factor authentication (MFA). All users must belong to a provisioned organization and are subject to administrative security policies enforced through the KSGC Enterprise Console.

KSGC client applications run on customer-managed devices and include web, desktop, and mobile clients. An optional Keeper Enterprise Bridge supports automated user provisioning and deprovisioning from customer Active Directory environments.

Keeper Security has no remote access to customer devices or vault contents. The only user information accessible to Keeper is limited to email address, device type, and subscription metadata.
KSGC is supported by a dedicated Information Security Operations team and a 24x7 third-party Security Operations Center (SOC).

Bugcrowd for Government (BCGOV) is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform providing on-demand offensive security testing capabilities delivered through a vetted community of security researchers. The platform enables customers to establish and manage structured security programs, including Vulnerability Disclosure Programs (VDP), Bug Bounties, and Penetration Testing. BCGOV includes a centralized management portal for scope definition, asset management, and researcher engagement, a triage pipeline for vulnerability validation and severity rating, and APIs for integrating validated findings into customer remediation workflows.

Drawing on decades of experience and innovation in IT security risk management supporting NIST 800-53, Risk Management Framework (RMF), Authority to Operate (ATO) maintenance, and grants and safety compliance for numerous federal agencies, Longevity developed Riskuity as a transformative tool to help organizations meet their regulatory responsibilities more effectively and efficiently. 

Hosted in the AWS FedRAMP-certified GovCloud environment, Riskuity is engineered with security and compliance at its core, leveraging best-in-class cloud-native services for performance, availability, and protection of sensitive data.

Multi-Channel Engagement: Natural language conversations via voice, web, and SMS channels with seamless handoff capabilities.
Flexible API Integration: Connect to various data sources and third-party systems for real-time information retrieval and processing.
Compliance-First Architecture: Built-in audit trails, conversation recording transcript generation, and comprehensive logging for regulatory requirements.
Scalable Multi-Tenant Infrastructure: Supporting enterprise, government agencies, and commercial applications.

Security & Compliance Features

End-to-end encryption for all data in transit and at rest.
Comprehensive audit logging of all system interactions and decision chains.
Role-based access control with multi-factor authentication.
Automated quality assurance through scorecard systems.
Human-in-the-loop oversight capabilities.

Platform Capabilities

Custom AI agent development for specific business processes.
Integration with public APIs (e.g., insurance quoting).
Workflow automation and intelligent routing.
Real-time transcription and analytics.
Bias monitoring and performance evaluation tools.

Our platform provides organizations with the tools to create transparent, auditable, and efficient AI-powered customer interactions while maintaining strict security and compliance standards required for sensitive data handling.

products.description:
The Get A-Head Platform is delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering using a multi-tenant SAAS cloud computing environment. A cloud-based SaaS platform deployed in AWS GovCloud that enables secure real-time audio communication and content delivery for behavioral health and training use cases. It is available to federal, state, local, and tribal governments, as well as research institutions and federal contractors.

To simplify the tracking, delivery, and administration of clinical training Get A-Head provides institutions with a digital ecosystem that centralizes and optimizes the interactions between supervisors, counseling trainees, and those seeking mental health support. 

Developed through the lens of both those seeking and providing mental health care, Get A-Head supports efficient communication between supervisor, trainee, and client, facilitate access to notes, and recorded sessions, across all devices (i.e., smartphones, laptops, desktops, and tablets) in a secure HIPAA & PIPEDA compliant environment.

The service includes authentication, user access management, secure media distribution, and activity logging through AWS native services, Keycloak, and internal microservices. All components are containerized and orchestrated using AWS services, and encryption is applied end-to-end using FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules. The authorization boundary includes the application services, identity and access management, storage, logging, monitoring, and backup components required to operate the platform in AWS GovCloud.

UKG provides a Workforce Operating Platform which helps organizations in every industry turn data into decisions that elevate productivity, culture, and the customer experience. UKG includes SMB, mid-market, and enterprise applications for human capital management, payroll, and workforce management. UKG Pro Workforce Management is a cloud-based offering for U.S. federal, state, and local government centers, as well as U.S. government contractors, and Federally Funded Research Development Centers (FFRDCs). UKG Pro Workforce Management is a complete WFM enterprise offerings, including the following cloud solutions: Timekeeping (hourly and salaried), Absence Management (Accruals, Leave, and Absence), Scheduling, Advanced Scheduling, and Forecasting, Long-term Workforce Planning, Activity Management, Data Collection & UDM, Labor Compliance, Global Payroll, Payroll Integration, Employee Self-Service, Workforce Analytics & Reporting, and Employee Engagement & Mobile tools. Also available are UKG Services, providing education, tools, training, and expertise to maximize their use of UKG Pro Workforce Management.

ArcSuite AI (formerly ArcProcure) is delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering using a multi-tenant, government-only community cloud environment hosted in AWS GovCloud (US). It is available to U.S. federal agencies, state and local governments, tribal entities, and government contractors authorized to operate within AWS GovCloud (US).
The authorized service provides capabilities for procurement Workflow to include AI assisted document analysis and generation, document review, approval workflows, and source selection. The authorization boundary includes the web application, backend microservices, workflow engine, API services, encrypted data storage, audit logging functions, and the associated network and security infrastructure hosted in AWS GovCloud (US).

products.cso: Managed Access Gateway with Managed Microsoft 365
products.description:
Exostar's Managed Access Gateway (MAG) with Managed Microsoft 365 (MM365) service provides a Microsoft Office 365 GCC High subscription hosting Teams and SharePoint, with Exostar performing the administrative and authentication controls. The customer retains control of their user lists, adding and removing personnel as needed, while Exostar handles security monitoring and common account monitoring duties. Exostar also provides the basic management and monitoring functions for the subscription. Customers may engage services for single-tenant or multi-tenant services.
 
MAG is Exostar?s comprehensive portal solution for registration, account management, and authentication as a means of providing controlled access to Exostar hosted applications and applications located within other enterprise domains in a B2B environment. MAG enables enterprises to provide controlled access to controlled information and applications by offering a standard deployment approach that ensures a single sign-on (SSO) federation infrastructure that can be seamlessly extended to support integrations.  MAG supports delegated administration, such that external organizations administrators (external to the relying party) can do full life cycle management of identities/account management in their domain of control. 
 
Exostar's Managed Microsoft 365 service provides a Microsoft Office 365 GCC High subscription hosting Teams and SharePoint, with Exostar performing the administrative and authentication controls. The customer retains control of which users are invited to join individual Teams, adding and removing personnel as needed, while Exostar handles security monitoring and common account monitoring duties. Exostar also provides the management and monitoring functions for the subscription. Customers may engage services for single-tenant or multi-tenant services.

products.description:
IntelliGRC is an AI-powered, all US-based and developed FedRAMP-aligned multi-tenant Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) SaaS platform purpose-built for Service Providers (MSPs/MSSPs), cybersecurity consultants, and regulated organizations that must achieve, manage and maintain audit-ready compliance at scale. The platform combines asset-centric scoping, context-aware control mapping, intelligent automation, bidirectional cross tenant shared implementations, and practitioner-trained AI features to streamline assessments, evidence collection, ongoing monitoring, and compliance program management across multiple frameworks. 

Designed with deep operational expertise from cybersecurity assessors and compliance practitioners, IntelliGRC enables service providers to deliver consistent, repeatable, and high-caliber compliance outcomes for their customers while reducing manual effort and SME dependency. IntelliGRC is widely used by organizations across the Defense Industrial Base (DIB), research institutions, and service providers supporting CMMC, NIST SP 800-171, NIST CSF, HIPAA, ISO 27001, SOC 2, FedRAMP, etc. Used in over 30+ CMMC L2 Certification Assessments.

A fully multi-tenant architecture allows providers to centrally manage multiple customer environments, distribute governance content, standardize workflows, and operationalize compliance-as-a-service (GRCaaS). IntelliGRC?s integrated AI assistant?trained on IntelliGRC methodologies and cybersecurity compliance best practices?supports evidence analysis, control interpretation, gap evaluation, and documentation generation. Our mission is to make authentic, tangible, actionable, high-caliber, and scalable cybersecurity compliance accessible.

Gov Cloud Secure is PSI?s secure platform for the registration, scheduling, delivery, administration, and reporting of computer-based assessments for federal agencies and other government organizations. The system supports assessment programs used for licensure, certification, workforce selection, and credentialing.
 
The Gov Cloud Secure FedRAMP authorization boundary includes candidate registration and scheduling components; secure assessment delivery applications; test session administration components; data storage and processing services; and reporting capabilities accessible to authorized agency users. The system is hosted within a government-authorized cloud infrastructure environment.

products.3pao_name: A-LIGN Compliance and Security, Inc. dba A-LIGN
products.description: Kaseware for U.S. Government is delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering using a multi-tenant hybrid cloud computing environment. The service is available to U.S. defense and federal civilian agencies, as well as state, local, and tribal government organizations. Kaseware is a cloud-based investigative and case management system designed to support the collection, management, analysis, and secure sharing of investigative, incident, and intelligence-related information. Kaseware provides authorized users with a configurable platform to create, manage, store, search, and analyze structured and unstructured data associated with cases, investigations, incidents, evidence, tasks, and related entities. Kaseware is deployed using a cloud-native, microservices-based architecture hosted within a Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) compliant cloud environment. The system supports both multi-tenant and single-tenant deployment models, depending on customer requirements, and includes logical separation of tenant data enforced through access controls and configuration.

Net-Inspect is a global, multi-tenant Software as a Service providing an Enterprise Quality Management system for aerospace, defense, medical, oil & gas, and general product manufacturing. Net-Inspect customers include very large enterprise customers, large multi-site manufacturers, midsize manufacturing sites, and small independent detail part manufacturing sites. The system is based, as much as possible, on aerospace standards such as the First Article Inspection AS9102 standard maintained by the International Aerospace Quality Group (IAQG). In addition, companies such as medical device manufacturing and general product companies use aerospace-based standard features for their manufacturing. 
In general, Net-Inspect is an online system that captures data provided by part manufacturing companies. Data is entered in one of four methods; online web form entry, uploading Excel templates, published data from industry partner companies, and interfaces with customer enterprise companies. Net-Inspect is not the source for manufacturers to receive customer proprietary information, but it is the long-term repository for the resulting manufacturer data.

The CSO provides web-based and API-based access to workflow orchestration, job execution, and results visualization capabilities. The CSO includes components for user authentication and role-based access control, audit logging, secure data transfer, encryption of data at rest and in transit, workflow execution management, and administrative monitoring. products.description:
Island Enterprise Browser is a Government-Community cloud environment that resides in the AWS GovCloud West (Oregon) region. Island leverages the UberEther IAM Advantage (IAMA) PaaS for implementation, management, and monitoring of the AWS GovCloud infrastructure housing Island Enterprise components and inherits FedRAMP High control environments from both AWS and UberEther IAMA.
Island Enterprise Browser has been implemented on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Services (EKS) to provide a microservices-based architecture. The production environment includes applications, operating systems, and AWS FedRAMP-authorized resources and services that support this architecture. The major components include:
Web services for access control as well as compute nodes on UberEther-managed instances managed by EKS
Access control for customer users to the front end through HTTPS (TLSv1.2 at a minimum)
Access control for internal Island Enterprise Browser users to the back end through privileged AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) user access from AWS Workspaces instances

Databases/Storage:
MySQL databases through Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS) as the primary data store? 
Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) for static content and object storage 
AWS CloudFront for managed caching? 

Networking:
Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with public and private subnets 
Route tables for traffic flow between subnets? 
AWS Firewall and Security groups, acting as virtual firewalls and access control lists 
Amazon EKS and application load balancers (ALBs) 
Amazon Route53 for external and internal DNS 
AWS Certification Manager for public certificates 
AWS Workspaces for remote internal access to the environment 
AWS Internet Gateway and NAT Gateway for access between VPCs 

UberEther-Provided AWS Services for monitoring, management, logging, authentication, and support of the system environment and components

Riverbed Platform for Government (RP4G) is delivered as a Software as a Service (SaaS) offering within a secure, multi-tenant, government-only cloud environment and is designed to provide visibility into network performance, application behavior, and end-user experience across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid infrastructures.

The platform integrates network performance monitoring, application observability, and digital experience monitoring capabilities into a unified architecture. The platform specializes in collecting and analyzing telemetry from network devices, applications, endpoints, servers, and cloud resources to support centralized dashboards, reporting, and intelligent analytics. It enables IT operations teams to identify performance degradation, investigate service disruptions, and conduct root-cause analysis, supporting reduced mean time to detection and resolution.

The SaaS environment incorporates role-based access controls, data segregation, encryption in transit and at rest, and audit logging to align with government security and compliance requirements, while supporting scalable data ingestion, analysis, and operational oversight for distributed environments.

products.3pao_name: A-LIGN Compliance and Security, Inc. dba A-LIGN
products.description:
Butterfly Network?s Inc.?s Butterfly iQ+ / iQ3 Ultrasound System/ Compass AI (aka Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System) enables universal access to superior medical imaging, making high quality ultrasound easy-to-use, globally accessible, and intelligently connected.

Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System is the only ultrasound transducer that can perform ?whole-body imaging? with a single handheld probe using semiconductor technology. Connected to a mobile phone or tablet, it is powered by Butterfly?s proprietary Ultrasound-on-Chip? technology and harnesses the advantages of AI to deliver advanced imaging that is easy-to-use and improves patient outcomes.

The primary function of the Butterfly App is general-purpose diagnostic imaging, for use by qualified and trained healthcare professionals to enable the visualization and measurement of anatomical structures within the human body. The app is a free download from the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. The app and a vetted Butterfly account are required to use the Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System.

The Butterfly Cloud is hosted by Amazon Web Services (AWS) in multi availability zone fashion with cross-region data replication.

The development environment is where the initial software creation and code revision occurs. It's an area for developers to freely work on new updates without affecting the live application.

The staging environment, also known as the Quality Assurance (QA) environment, is the intermediary step where the software is tested in conditions that closely mirror the production environment. This is where thorough checks are conducted before any release.

The production environment is the live setting where the software is fully deployed for use by the end-users. It is the final and most stable environment, designed for uptime and reliability.

Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System offers two primary services: Public Cloud and Private or Enterprise Cloud:

Public Cloud: Designed for small groups, such as a team using the Butterfly web application, this service allows for the creation of an organization within the cloud. Team members can invite colleagues to join their organization. This multi-tenant approach enables different organizations, like those from various hospitals, to coexist simultaneously within the same public cloud infrastructure.

Private Cloud: An upgrade from the public cloud, this service provides a unique domain tailored for specific institutions, such as hospitals, which may establish separate organizations for each department. Additionally, Enterprise Cloud offers enhanced features like Single Sign-On (SSO), allowing users to access the cloud services with their existing institutional credentials. Like the public cloud, multi-tenant approach enables different organizations, like those from various hospitals, to coexist simultaneously within the same private cloud infrastructure.

The Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System uses industry standard third-party interconnections to provide robust and up to date security tools to protect the integrity of the Butterfly Ultrasound Probe System and related information.

Aeroplicity provides frictionless operating software built for Aerospace & Defense. Our platform simplifies maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO), strengthens supply-chain logistics, and streamlines cybersecurity compliance. By embedding DFARS, CMMC, ITAR, and other DoD mandates directly into workflows, we reduce the burden of compliance while enabling faster, more secure collaboration across OEMs, primes, and subcontractors. Built on FedRAMP-authorized infrastructure with blockchain-backed auditability, Aeroplicity is AI-native?applying advanced automation and decision support to deliver reliable, explainable outcomes compliantly. Our advantage is rapid onboarding, immutable compliance records, and deep A&D domain expertise?helping contractors of every size operate with the speed and assurance demanded by today?s defense ecosystem.
products.authorizing_agency_id: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program

products.description:
Canopy is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) document intelligence platform provided by Ripcord in a single-tenant Government-only cloud environment. The service enables federal agencies to digitize, process, classify, and extract data from structured and unstructured documents at scale.

Canopy integrates robotics-enabled document ingestion, machine learning and large language model-based data extraction, and workflow orchestration to automate document processing pipelines. The platform supports document classification, OCR/ICR processing, metadata extraction, validation workflows, and delivery of structured outputs to downstream agency systems.

The system operates within a FedRAMP High authorization boundary hosted in Google Cloud Platform and leverages stackArmor?s Armory General Support System for security services including monitoring, configuration management, and continuous monitoring. Authentication is provided through Okta Government High Cloud with multi-factor authentication.

Canopy provides secure ingestion, processing, validation, and export of document data while maintaining auditability, chain-of-custody tracking, and integration with agency systems using standard data formats such as XML, JSON, and PDF.

An authorization boundary provides a diagrammatic illustration of a CSO?s internal services,
components, and other devices, along with connections to external services and systems.
Please note that external services include external cloud services that are not FedRAMP
Authorized, corporate shared services, and the external entities to which the system must
connect to receive updates for products installed within the system boundary.

An authorization boundary accounts for all federal information, data, and metadata that flow
through a CSO. If the CSO has strong configuration management and change management
built into the system development life cycle, the development environment can be outside the CSO boundary. This means that there is a 3PAO validated, reproducible and effective way to make service changes without impacting the production environment.

The authorization boundary diagram displays all in-scope system components for the Ironclad FedRAMP environment and is depicted with a prominent red border. All components outside of the authorization boundary are shown externally to the red border. The authorization boundary encompasses the Ironclad application and supporting infrastructure hosted entirely within GCP in the us-central1 region. All components depicted within the boundary directly support the operation, security, monitoring, and administration of the CLM system.

Within the authorization boundary, the FedRAMP environment consists of customer-facing
application services, internal application services, and data storage components deployed on GCP. Application components communicate with one another using Google-managed
networking services and all internal data flows are encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Data at rest within the authorization boundary is encrypted using customer-specific encryption keys and uses FIPS 140-2-validated cryptographic modules or higher where available.

Ingress and egress to the authorization boundary are controlled through a load balancer and
WAF that provide a single logical entry point for external connections. The WAF is configured to restrict and allow traffic based on approved ports, protocols, and services defined in the PPSM list. Network traffic entering or leaving the environment traverses these boundary protection mechanisms, and all external communications are encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.

Development and test environments are depicted and logically separated from the production environment. Access to development and test environments is restricted to authorized Ironclad personnel. Backup storage is provided through Google-managed backup services within the authorized environment and is depicted on the diagram. Backup data is encrypted at rest and in transit using FIPS-validated cryptographic modules.

IBM Platform Services provides core services for managing devices, applications, content, identity & access management, end-to-end visibility, dynamic resource allocation, and improved operational risk and resiliency. These services work together to modernize device management, IAM, observability, application resource monitoring, and providing clients with AI-driven recommendations and tips that are prioritized and customized for your applications. 

IBM MaaS360 for Government is a comprehensive, cloud-based unified endpoint management and security platform for devices, applications and content built on a secure multi-tenant architecture. Government agencies use MaaS360 to protect data and optimize productivity, enabling employees to work anytime and anywhere through trusted mobile interactions. MaaS360 offers device management and security without the added costs and complexities of deploying hardware and software, dealing with intricate configurations, and dedicating additional resources to support the solution on an ongoing basis. MaaS360 allows administrators to manage and enable security on smartphones, tablets and laptops and increase end users? productivity by enabling secure access to their enterprise data on their endpoint devices. 

IBM Verify for Government is an Identity-as-a-Service (IDaaS) platform enabling organizations to secure and modernize experiences for workforce and citizens. IBM Verify provides modern Single-sign-on (SSO) using standard protocols (SAML, OIDC, OAuth, etc.), supports legacy protocols and access to thousands of popular cloud service applications, and in-house applications, with hundreds of pre-built templates for faster integration. IBM Verify manages the lifecycle of users and groups through a scalable and elastic directory, provides granular, attribute-based policies, and supports multi-factor authentication. Government agencies can provide a digital first experience for secure and frictionless authentication for citizens. 

IBM Instana for Government is a leading full-stack observability tool that automatically and continuously delivers the highest-fidelity data in one-second granularity, with end-to-end distributed tracing of every call or request, and provides a graphing model with dynamic maps to show you how everything is related. IBM Instana for Government allows you to see the exact data you want in the context you need to quickly determine the cause of an issue and take immediate action to fix it. Government agencies can monitor their entire stack with end-to-end visibility across shared services and gain real-time contextual understanding to find and fix problems faster.

IBM Turbonomic for Government is the leading Application Resource Management (ARM) solution that dynamically allocates resources in real-time boosting efficiency, minimizing cloud expense, and preventing downtime. IBM Turbonomic for Government delivers continuously optimized application resource performance across hybrid multi-cloud environments providing intelligence automation and proactive resource management that scales existing resources efficiently; preemptive analytics to help maximize resource utilization; dynamic resource allocation; and seamless integration across on premises, public, private, hybrid and multiple cloud domains.

IBM Concert for Government is the connective tissue that harmonizes data from disparate tools and environments, transforming it into actionable knowledge aimed at improving operational risk and resiliency and freeing up teams to focus more on innovation. IBM Concert for Government provides AI-driven recommendations and tips that are prioritized and customized for your applications. Get automated data analysis from disparate systems and tools, identify operational issues before they escalate, increase efficiency with Concert?s recommendations and automation of repetitive tasks. Government agencies can uncover crucial insights about their operations and get application-specific recommendations for improvement.

Citrix for Government (Citrix Cloud Government) is a sovereign cloud environment that is isolated from and operates independently of Citrix Cloud. These services enable U.S. government agencies and other customers in support of Public Sector to use Citrix Cloud Government in accordance with applicable regulatory and compliance requirements. Citrix Cloud Government operates within defined geographic boundaries and stores and replicates services and data to deliver Citrix cloud services. Citrix uses multiple public or private cloud environments located in one or more U.S. states to provide these services. 

Citrix for Government consists of the following components: 

The Citrix Cloud platform provides centralized administration for Citrix services. It enables customers to create, manage, and deploy workspaces?including applications and data?to end users from a single console, while connecting to customer resources across any cloud or infrastructure of their choice. 

Citrix DaaS (Desktop as a Service) provides virtualization capabilities that support the management of virtual machines, desktops, applications, and security controls, while enabling users to access resources from any location and on a wide range of devices. This hybrid cloud solution provides secure access to virtual applications, desktops, and data across cloud and on-premises infrastructure.
