products.description:
CrestPoint Environmental Management Service (CEMS) Platform 
 In accordance with FedRAMP requirements and policies, CrestPoint Solutions has completed the FedRAMP review process and is ready to support agency requests.
The CrestPoint Environmental Management Service (CEMS) Platform or the Unified FME Platform is delivered as a SaaS offering using a multi-tenant Public Cloud computing environment. It is a multipurpose modular system available to hospitals, healthcare facilities, data centers, public commercial facilities, federal, state, local, and tribal governments, as well as research institutions, federal contractors, and government contractors. The system is used by mission-critical facilities such as hospitals and data centers for managing operational and facility data including infrastructure data and asset management. 
 
For VA hospitals and other healthcare facilities, CEMS helps the EVS team manage infection prevention efforts. It provides the necessary oversight to manage and monitor EVS operations in a consolidated approach for the following EVS scopes of service: managing and tracking quality inspections and deficiencies for performance improvement, tracking bed turnover, reporting and analyzing textile care production data and cost of operations, managing day-to-day EVS key performance metrics, managing EVS key performance indicators (KPI), and incorporating integrated pest management (IPM) actions taken to address identified/reported deficiencies.
 
CEMS was uniquely designed to efficiently help EVS Leadership manage regulatory mandates such as the VHA Directive 1850 and others like The Joint Commission (TJC) Environment of Care (EC) Standards, CDC Sanitization Guidelines, CMS Infection Control, EPA/OSHA Medical Waste Regulations, Healthcare Laundry Accreditation Council (HLAC) Standards, and Integrated Pest Management (IPM) Protocols. These are mandated directives. Failure to adequately track infection prevention efforts, the various deficiencies, and the management of the EVS Key Performance Indicators (KPI) will directly affect the health and well-being of veterans and patients. A possible consequence is an infection outbreak or even an epidemiological outbreak.
 
As a result of these digital records, EVS staff and management have access to improved metrics that help put patients first, providing them with much better quality of care, faster response times and improved safety. Electronic records enable EVS teams to address patient needs in real time, eliminating wait times and backlog in EVS operations.
For Data Centers and infrastructure management, CEMS provides an integrated detailed specification and graphical representation of equipment for capacity planning. CEMS is used for power, space, and power requirements. It facilitates the automation and verification of scheduled and preventative maintenance, true-ups and end-of-life notifications. CEMS addresses today?s critical infrastructure challenges such as: floor space management, network connectivity, asset management, live power and environmental monitoring, critical AC - DC power systems, integrates with live data, Intelligent business analytics. It has the capability for multiple site management, measures PUE and/or DCiE, capacity planning, an intuitive, web-based graphical user interface (GUI), online collaboration and scheduling tools, easily manages work orders and resources, and provides a centralized repository for site drawings.
Our commitment to FedRAMP requirements reflects our dedication to protecting sensitive data and upholding the highest security standards.

Black Kite is delivered as a SaaS offering using a multi-tenant public cloud computing environment. It is available to the public, federal, state, local, and tribal governments, as well as research institutions, federal contractors, government contractors etc. Black Kite offers a separate configuration for federal/public sector customers to maintain the highest possible level of information security. Black Kite provides a defensive platform that spans the entirety of the third-party risk management life cycle for cyber risk professionals looking to quantify and maintain visibility of their risk exposure.
Black Kite?s core application, is a multi-user, transaction-based application suite that gives a three-dimensional risk picture of a company and its vendors through:
? A cyber risk technical assessment.
? A factor analysis of information risk (FAIR) analysis (the probable financial impact of a breach caused by a vendor).
? An external estimate of compliance (assessing that vendors have appropriate policies and processes in place).
? Executives get an easy-to-understand ?Cyber Risk Report? with letter grade scores.
? IT security teams can drill down to the technical details behind each risk category.
? Risks/vulnerabilities are prioritized by severity so that security engineers can quickly identify critical issues and mitigate them.
? Industry benchmarks and comparisons are provided to help identify trends and pinpoint areas for improvement.
? Grading is based on industry standards and best practices (NIST, MITRE CVSS, FAIR, etc.)

The GO.gov system is a multi-component System of Systems (SoS) designed to provide an integrated suite of travel, expense, integration, notification, and reporting services in support of Federal agencies. GO.gov?s core purpose is to streamline and standardize federal travel and expense operations while maintaining compliance with federal security and privacy regulations. GO.gov integrates several commercial Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) and Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offerings with secure hosting environments within AWS GovCloud (US) and AWS East/West commercial regions to provide a unified, end-to-end business capability.

911inform is a secure, cloud-based emergency response and situational intelligence platform designed for federal and enterprise environments requiring real-time visibility and coordinated response during critical incidents. Delivered within a FedRAMP-aligned SaaS architecture, the platform provides a centralized command-and-control interface for managing emergencies across facilities and agencies.

At its core is 911inform?s patented Location Discovery Solution (LDS), a Ray Baum?s Act-compliant technology that delivers precise, dynamic location data for 911 calls across PBX, softphone, and mobile devices. The platform enhances emergency response through patented interactive mapping, real-time cellular 9-1-1 call detection with location reporting, and automated multi-channel notifications.

911inform enables secure communications and interoperability between organizations and first responders, delivering real-time situational intelligence to improve coordination and decision-making.

Designed for scalability and rapid deployment, 911inform integrates with existing telephony, security, and IT infrastructure while maintaining role-based access control, auditability, and secure data handling.

 

SERVICES:

RAY BAUM's Act Compliance

Kari?s Law Compliance

Cellular 9-1-1 Call reporting

Interactive mapping with routing

Notification and messaging services

First Responder Coordination

Emergency Location Services

CLEAR1 is CLEAR?s secure identity platform used to perform remote identity proofing for federal agencies and highly regulated environments. Identity is confirmed by correlating biometric signals, government-issued credentials, device attributes, and authoritative and credible evidence sources in real time.

CLEAR1 protects high-stakes workflows such as onboarding, account creation and recovery, and sensitive data access. The platform is policy-driven and aligned to the NIST Digital Identity Guidelines, including IAL2 and AAL2, and is approved by the Kantara Initiative as meeting these standards. Our comprehensive security program meets high standards for data protection and privacy, and is built to protect personal information and keep users in control. By verifying the person ? not just the documents ? CLEAR1 helps federal programs advance trust and protect their users at scale.

Everbridge Critical Event Management GovCloud is a suite of applications that empower the government community to detect, manage, and respond to critical events affecting personnel, facilities, and operations. The end-to-end platform integrates AI-assisted capabilities across applications such as mass notification and incident communication, real-time risk intelligence, geospatial common operating picture, crisis response management, and workflow automation to help agencies know earlier, respond faster, and improve continuously when managing threats and emergencies. 
 
Communications: 
Robust mass notification and incident communications capabilities enable personnel and stakeholders to share relevant information and situational awareness. This enables organizations to efficiently execute Emergency Mass Notification, Recall/Relocation, Personnel Accountability, Traveler and Lone Worker Safety, and targeted Emergency Response Team Coordination. The platform supports common alerting protocols and is capable of integrating with other alerting modalities.  Notification delivery is provided through multiple channels including:  text messages, emails, voice calls, mobile app notifications, desktop alerting.  It provides two-way communication capabilities enabling recipients to acknowledge receipt, confirm safety, request assistance, and/or receive location-specific information during emergencies. 

Visual Command Center: 
Visual Command Center (VCC) is the visualization and orchestration engine for Everbridge Critical Event Management GovCloud. VCC delivers a centralized common operating picture ? integrating high fidelity contact, asset, and geospatial data ? to enhance situational awareness and incident response. Through a single, unified view, organizations gain real-time threat intelligence and collaborative response capabilities, enabling them to mitigate or eliminate the impact of critical events on an unprecedented scale.

Risk Intelligence: 
The Everbridge Risk Intelligence Monitoring Center (RIMC) analyzes thousands of trustworthy, vetted, and hyper-local data sources ? across over 100 risk categories ? using machine-learning and AI technology, complemented by an experienced team of global risk analysts. Everbridge?s real-time alerting streamlines your organization?s ability to monitor and analyze worldwide events, dramatically increasing your ability to respond to risks that threaten your people, organization, supply chain. 

Response Management: 
Response Management digitizes response plans, shares documentation, tracks tasks and streamlines communications workflows for coordinated response efforts during crisis and other critical events. The platform supports collaborative decision-making and provides real-time status dashboards, situation reports (SITREPS) and after-action reporting (AAR) capabilities to provide full auditability and identify improvement opportunities for future events.

Everbridge Critical Event Management GovCloud is designed to be highly secure and scalable, supporting federal agencies in protecting personnel, maintaining operational continuity, and strengthening organizational resilience across diverse threat scenarios including natural disasters, IT/cybersecurity incidents, civil unrest, workplace safety events, and more.

Cyera for Government is an agentless, cloud-native SaaS data security platform.  Cyera for Government provides continuous visibility, AI-powered data classification, and protection for federal data across hybrid-cloud, SaaS, and on-premises environments. The system functions as a centralized data security control plane, enabling agencies to discover sensitive information, assess risk posture, and enforce security policies in real-time. By unifying Data Security Posture Management (DSPM) with advanced Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and Artificial Intelligence Security Posture Management (AI-SPM), the platform provides the foundation for securing the federal AI lifecycle.
Cyera for Government enables the automated discovery and contextual classification of structured and unstructured data. It utilizes an AI-native classification engine to identify Federal Information, including Personally Identifiable Information (PII), Protected Health Information (PHI), and Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI).
Beyond discovery, the platform delivers automated risk remediation and continuous monitoring to close security gaps before they are exploited. By analyzing data access patterns and permissions, Cyera identifies over-privileged users and "shadow data" that bypasses traditional controls. This proactive approach allows agencies to implement Zero Trust Data Access (ZTDA) architectures, ensuring that sensitive federal assets remain resilient against insider threats and external breaches.
Cyera for Government is specifically engineered to streamline regulatory compliance with federal mandates such as NIST 800-53, CMMC, HIPAA, and FISMA. The platform provides out-of-the-box reporting and audit trails that simplify the assessment and authorization process.

Wraithwatch is a Software-as-a-Service cybersecurity platform built on an intelligent security data fabric. The platform ingests data from across the customer's security and IT stack ? endpoint detection and response, SIEM, identity providers, vulnerability scanners, cloud infrastructure, and more ? through agentless, read-only API integrations. No agents are deployed on customer endpoints. Ingested data is normalized through deterministic entity resolution into a continuously updated entity graph that models every device, user, permission, vulnerability, configuration, and relationship in the environment. This unified data fabric eliminates tool-by-tool data silos and provides a persistent, queryable model of the customer's environment that powers all downstream platform capabilities.

Continuous Governance, Risk, and Compliance: Wraithwatch replaces periodic, point-in-time compliance assessments with continuous GRC that recomputes every few minutes and immediately upon auto-detection of environmental changes. The platform mathematically computes every viable attack path based on current topology, configurations, permissions, and verified reachability. When the environment changes ? a new CVE, a granted permission, a configuration drift ? the platform recomputes risks including second-, third-, and fourth-order cascading effects. Compliance posture, risk exposure, and control effectiveness are maintained as living measurements against the entity graph rather than static snapshots. Agencies can execute neutralization plans directly from Wraithwatch or deploy compensating detections where paths cannot be eliminated.

Threat Hunting: Autonomous reasoning workers explore SIEM indexes and security telemetry, identify anomalies and threat precursors, and surface findings with supporting evidence. Analysts issue natural language queries and the platform translates, hunts, pivots across data sources, and performs cross-index correlation at query runtime without requiring pre-mapped data schemas. Results convert into forward-looking detection rules.

Threat Advisory Correlation: Wraithwatch continuously correlates incoming threat intelligence ? including newly published CVEs, active exploitation advisories, novel attack techniques, supply chain compromise reporting, and other non-CVE threat disclosures ? against the current state of the entity graph. When a new threat is published, the platform automatically evaluates the customer's environment and produces an "am I affected" assessment identifying specific impacted assets, exposure conditions, and recommended mitigations. This analysis incorporates the full environmental context ? network reachability, compensating controls, privilege relationships, and configuration state ? to determine actual exploitability, not just the presence of a vulnerable component.

Control Plan Generation: Agency personnel describe a desired security outcome, and Wraithwatch evaluates every relevant setting across every integrated tool to generate a control plan. Plans are executable in full, one step at a time, or through phased rollouts with test groups. All actions are auditable with rollback support.

All adversarial analysis runs against the digital twin. No live exploits touch production infrastructure. The platform operates in read-only mode by default; write-mode actions require explicit customer authorization and human confirmation. Wraithwatch supports cloud-hosted deployment on FedRAMP-authorized infrastructure and fully on-premise deployment for air-gapped and CUI environments.

xAI?s Grok Enterprise for Government delivers frontier AI capabilities purpose-built for U.S. federal agencies, now available in a secure, compliant environment. Powered by the most advanced Grok family of models, it combines unmatched reasoning, real-time knowledge integration (including secure enterprise search), multi-agent collaboration, multimodal understanding (text, code, images, and video), massive context windows, and powerful API/agentic workflows.

ESChat for Government is a hardened version of the ESChat broadband communication, collaboration, and interoperability platform. ESChat is a Secure Broadband Push-to-Talk (PTT) communication system that facilitates secure inter-agency PTT communication, multimedia messaging, and a Common Operating Picture (COP) within and between agencies for mutual-aid or tactical purposes. ESChat for Government also supports external interfaces including Land Mobile Radio (LMR), dispatch consoles, and logging recorders

There are three main components that make up this offering:

- The ESChat portal is a browser-based application which allows customers to manage all aspects of their network including managing accounts, devices, groups, integrations,live and historical location display, etc.
- The ESChat clients are mobile applications (Android, iOS, Linux), Land Mobile Radio (LMR) gateways, and PC Client applications that communicate using the ESChat protocol. The ESChat clients are the functional endpoints that the users of the system interact with to perform Push-to-Talk voice and multimedia messaging communications with one another.
- The ESChat back-end services provide ESChat users with inter-carrier, inter-agency, inter-system, and inter-vendor interoperability all managed and controlled within the authorization boundary, ensuring ESChat is the only solution needed to provide reliable, secure, and interoperable communications.

ComplySyncATO is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) automation platform designed to support continuous authorization and monitoring activities within cloud environments. The Cloud Service Offering (CSO) enables organizations to manage compliance posture through automated validation, evidence collection, and reporting aligned with FedRAMP 20x requirements.

ComplySyncATO integrates with cloud services to collect operational data and supports integration with external systems and service providers, enabling aggregation of compliance data and a unified view of compliance posture across multiple environments. The service is designed to support low to moderate impact systems and enables organizations to demonstrate continuous security compliance in machine-readable and human-readable formats.

products.description:
The Adaptiva OneSite Platform enables its customers to autonomously manage the distribution of software and patches to endpoints across a customer?s enterprise, within the customer network, or across the Internet. The OneSite Platform consists of four primary components:
1)        A customer on-premises server operated and managed by customers that hosts an Adaptiva-developed OneSite application used to manage software distribution to the customer?s endpoints.
2)        A content delivery network (CDN) that enables customers to place software in the cloud for retrieval by managed customer endpoints across the Internet.
3)        Adaptiva Cloud Services Relay Servers that receive messages from a customer?s on-premises server and managed clients on the Internet. The relay servers facilitate communication between the customer server and the Internet-facing devices. 
4)        An Operations Manager (OpsMgr) server that is used by Adaptiva to manage OneSite cloud functionality and customer OneSite installation information.
The customer on-premises server provides functionality for the customer to manage the distribution of software to their endpoints. The server is owned and operated by the customer. Adaptiva support personnel do not have the ability to access or manage it. Software updates from Adaptiva are installed by the customer.
The Adaptiva OneSite Platform CDN is provided by Akamai, a FedRAMP-authorized Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) solution. The customer on-premises server running the OneSite application communicates with the CDN to upload the customer?s software that is in-turn accessed by the customer?s endpoints through commands conveyed by the relay server.
The relay server conveys command messages from a customer on-premises server to customer end points to direct the end points to download and install software. The relay server processes thousands of messages from on-premises servers to tens of thousands of customer endpoints. This server is hosted by DataBank, a FedRAMP-authorized IaaS and Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) provider.
An OpsMgr server is used for activating customer servers to be allowed to use relays for internet clients to obtain metadata and keys. The OpsMgr also receives and stores the messaging statistics for customer server and end points from relay servers. This OpsMgr server is hosted by DataBank.

Glytec?s Glucommander? is an FDA-cleared software-as-a-medical-device (SaMD), utilized for diabetes management and automated insulin titration. Glucommander is purpose-built to improve patient safety and clinical outcomes. Glucommander provides clinicians, hospitalists, and medical facilities with constant, data-driven dosing recommendations that stabilize blood glucose levels and decrease the incidences of hypoglycemia, length of stays and readmissions.

Midwatch is a secure enclave engineered for the protection of Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI). It provides Federal Agencies and the Defense Industrial Base (DIB) with a secure environment to handle and share sensitive data requiring moderate security controls. Through the use of Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI), Midwatch ensures strict logical separation between secure workloads and an organization?s broader enterprise information systems.

Midwatch enables secure, high-performance collaboration across several critical workflows, including:
Sensitive Data Collaboration: Secure co-authoring and collaboration on sensitive information and complex documentation stacks.
Advanced Engineering: Specialized support for CAD workflows and AI/ML GPU-accelerated workloads.
Software Engineering: Secure Software Development and DevSecOps via an integrated Gitflow platform, implemented in alignment with the Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF).
Operational Management: Integrated project management and secure task orchestration.
Remote Device Management: Midwatch ViPR provides a secure gateway between the VDI infrastructure and physical office or workshop floors, facilitating controlled and audited file transfers to engineering peripheral devices.

The Midwatch authorization boundary consists of a DevSecOps platform, VDI infrastructure, Midwatch ViPR remote access services, workflow automation, and vulnerability management tools, all hosted within a dedicated cloud organization. The environment is hardened through a segmented VPC architecture with Google Cloud Armor edge protection, the implementation of a Zero Trust Network Architecture (ZTNA) for all access requests, and robust data protection featuring centralized logging and Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK).

Midwatch is deployed as a government-community cloud within the Google Cloud Platform (GCP) using US-only data centers. The system utilizes a microservices-based architecture powered by Docker and K3s/K8s/GKE Autopilot.

Pepelwerk is a cloud-based workforce and talent management designed to automate the creation, maintenance, and updating of Talent Profiles. Pepelwerk supports the Cybersecurity Talent Management System (CTMS) by capturing CTMS-specific data not addressed by traditional Title 5 systems, such as career track/level, technical capabilities, qualifications, education/certifications, and assignment details. 
Pepelwerk? s primary objective is to streamline and modernize the management of CTMS Talent Profiles, ensuring accurate, timely, and consistent data capture for employees. The system is intended to: 
?        Reduce manual workload for HR specialists. 
?        Improve data accuracy and consistency. 
?        Enable supervisors to review and validate employee profile updates. 
?        Support annual updates during the Mission Impact Review (CTMS performance process). 
?        Pilot the solution with Entry and Developmental Career Track employees, with potential for future expansion. 
Pepelwerk provides a wide range of workforce and talent management services, including: 
For Individuals: 
?        Career exploration and transition support for those starting work life, seeking a career change, or transitioning from military to civilian employment. 
?        Access to internships and apprenticeships. 
?        Data-driven career decision tools. 
?        Career coaching (find or become a coach), and resources for parents of young adults. 
?        Community conversations and engagement for career development. 
For Organizations: 
?        AI and automation tools for agile talent management and workforce planning. 
?        Talent development marketplace for employee growth and recruitment. 
?        Community-driven data and predictive analytics. 
?        Impact dashboard modules for organizational measurement. 
?        Customizable talent marketplace creation. 
?        AI Career Assistant for employees, members, alumni, students, and citizens. 
?        Sponsorship opportunities for AI career assistants or educational initiatives. 
?        Workforce building tools to develop future hires and recruit based on skills and attributes. 
Course matching for student enrollment and real-time training and skills development.

Vanta Government Cloud is an end-to-end security and compliance platform powered by AI and automation. From continuous governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) and vendor risk management to questionnaire automation and Trust Center visibility, Vanta helps organizations streamline compliance across 35+ frameworks - including SOC 2, ISO 27001, FedRAMP, NIST 800-53. NIST CSF, CMMC, and more. With over 16,000 customers, Vanta is the continuous monitoring solution that helps organizations achieve and maintain a strong security posture.

products.description:
Phenom is an applied AI company committed to helping public agencies hire faster, develop better, and retain longer - all through the power of experience and intelligence. Our AI-powered talent experience platform connects every stakeholder in the talent journey - candidates, recruiters, employees, and hiring managers - to improve productivity, retention, and hiring outcomes.
Phenom for Government is delivered as a SaaS offering using a multi-tenant Government-only cloud computing environment. It is available to federal, state, local, and tribal governments, as well as research institutions, federal contractors, and government contractors. Built on a scalable, secure, and extensible architecture, Phenom?s platform enables public sector organizations to modernize how they attract, engage, and retain talent while ensuring security and privacy compliance. From intelligent career sites that improve the candidate experience to automated screening, scheduling, and onboarding, Phenom accelerates the hiring process while reducing administrative burden on HR teams.
Trusted by global enterprises, Phenom enables agencies and contractors to address today?s talent challenges including skill shortages, digital transformation, and workforce modernization with a unified platform built for speed, agility, and scale.

TrackVia Government is delivered as a multi-tenant Public SaaS cloud computing environment. TrackVia Government is a trusted low-code application platform empowering customers to rapidly build and deploy custom workflow and data management applications without writing code. Designed for operational teams, TrackVia Government enables fast, compliant digitization of critical processes like field inspections, asset tracking, and compliance audits. With native mobile apps, full offline functionality, and seamless integrations, TrackVia Government helps federal organizations eliminate manual work, increase visibility, and modernize operations securely and at scale.

Supply Chain Command (SCC) is a web based end-to-end, comprehensive solution that delivers quantifiable risk reduction across your entire third-party ecosystem, higher levels of efficiency to risk management processes, and greater visibility across your supply chain.
SCC drives cyber risk reduction at every stage of the third-party lifecycle, from sourcing & selection, on through ongoing monitoring and reassessment. The highly configurable solution set includes multiple levels of continuous monitoring based on vendor risk levels, and point-in-time risk assessments to support procurement and M&A decision making.

SCC provides visibility and prioritization for the entire third-party ecosystem via validated mappings of externally facing digital infrastructure and provides continuous monitoring of third parties with identification and notification of zero-day vulnerabilities in as little as 90 minutes following disclosure. SCC further provides on demand easy to understand snapshots of acquisition or procurement targets? cyber risk posture.

products.3pao_name: A-LIGN Compliance and Security, Inc. dba A-LIGN
products.description:
Moodle for Government is a FedRAMP? Ready SaaS Learning Management System (LMS) powered by Moodle Workplace, specifically engineered to meet the mission-critical training and compliance requirements of U.S. public sector agencies including federal, state, and local. Built on a foundation of "security by design," Moodle for Government provides a secure, scalable, and highly customizable environment for mandatory compliance training, workforce development, onboarding, and large-scale government training initiatives that evolves with your agency.
As our dedicated public sector solution built around Moodle?s enterprise learning platform, Moodle for Government enables agencies to modernize their learning ecosystems while maintaining the highest standards of control and accountability. This solution includes a wide variety of additional plugins to extend enterprise capabilities even further, and it is deployed in AWS GovCloud through our own proprietary hosting tool, which orchestrates our curated set of infrastructure components. Our solution is designed to optimize performance, reliability, and security while also offering a high level of autonomy, with dedicated per-client resources and client control of major-release upgrade scheduling.
Moodle for Government is committed to accessibility and meeting federal mandates. The platform is 508 and WCAG 2.2 AA compliant and supports over 20 languages, ensuring all personnel can access training regardless of location or ability. In addition to its FedRAMP Ready status, Moodle services in the U.S. are SOC 2 Type 2 compliant.
With robust functionality, Moodle for Government empowers teams to:
-Utilize rule-based automation to track certifications, manage renewals, and generate audit-ready reports.
-Deliver role-based learning paths tailored to specific job functions or mission requirements.
-Gain real-time visibility into training progress and skills gaps through customizable reporting dashboards.

products.3pao_name: A-LIGN Compliance and Security, Inc. dba A-LIGN
products.description: The ZeroEyes Federal (ZEF) threat detection solution is specifically engineered for federal agencies requiring strict adherence to security and data residency mandates. Utilizing advanced computer vision, the system provides continuous, real-time monitoring to identify visible firearms, including rifles and pistols, proactively alerting personnel before a shot is fired. Hosted within a FedRAMP Moderate Authorized environment

CMMC Space is a consulting firm specializing in cybersecurity and organizational transformation for the U.S. defense and national security community, with a strong focus on helping small businesses achieve Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) compliance. The company provides managed security services, including a CMMC-focused platform called "CMMC Space" that leverages Google Workspace to offer an affordable, all-in-one solution for compliance. Additionally, ATX Defense is an authorized CMMC Third-Party Assessor Organization (C3PAO), providing official assessments and gap analyses to help organizations prepare for and achieve certification.

GovDash is a cloud-native, multi-tenant SaaS platform purpose-built for public-sector business development. Delivered in a secure hybrid-cloud environment and accessed through any modern browser, it unifies the core work areas (Discover, Capture Cloud, Proposal Cloud, Contract Cloud, Word Assistant, and a secure Data Library) so federal contractors and subcontractors can manage the entire opportunity-to-award process from a single workspace.

Across these modules, AI-driven workflows accelerate each stage of the contracting lifecycle: Discover provides search and business development research tooling; Capture Cloud pinpoints relevant opportunities and guides solution architecture; Proposal Cloud automates compliance matrices, outlines, content drafting, and in-document graphics editing; Contract Cloud centralizes award data, CPARS, and status reporting; and the Data Library stores reusable credentials and documents. Together, these capabilities enable teams to find bids faster, craft higher-quality proposals with less manual effort, and maintain full visibility over contracts after award.

Magnet One delivers secure digital forensics and investigative analysis capabilities within a FedRAMP High authorized cloud environment, enabling U.S. federal agencies to ingest, process, analyze, and manage digital evidence in compliance with federal security requirements. The offering combines Magnet One?s advanced evidence review and collaboration features with a rigorously controlled architecture, documented security controls, and continuous monitoring aligned to FedRAMP standards, supporting mission operations while maintaining the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of sensitive federal data.

Halo is a multi-tenant cloud platform that provides a unified operating environment for service management, workflow automation, reporting, and compliance operations. The platform is designed to support organizations in managing operational processes, security workflows, and regulated service delivery from a single, integrated system.

For Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), Halo supports FedRAMP, CMMC, and DoD compliance programs by enabling compliance activities to be managed as part of day-to-day operations rather than as a separate workstream. Halo's GRC module provides functionality for vulnerability and risk management, Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) tracking, asset inventory, deviation management, OSCAL-based system documentation, and cross-framework control mapping. It also supports continuous monitoring packages, Key Security Indicator validation, and Ongoing Authorization deliverables in both human-readable and machine-readable formats.

In addition to GRC, the Halo platform supports broader enterprise service management capabilities, including IT Service Management (ITSM), Enterprise Service Management (ESM), Configuration Management (CMDB), and Service Level Management (SLM). These include core ITIL practices such as change management, incident management, user access management, self-service portals, automated task assignment and escalation, SLA tracking, and executive dashboards.

The Wallarm Federal Cloud (WFC) is a Software as a Service (SaaS) application programming interface (API) security platform that provides real-time traffic inspection and Web Application Firewall (WAF) capabilities for federal agencies. It is deployed in a single-tenant, Government-Only Cloud architecture on Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

Functionality and Use Case:
The system is designed to analyze incoming network traffic, mitigate malicious requests, and upload security metrics. The Wallarm Federal Cloud component processes this filtering node data, compiles custom security rules, builds API structure models, and provides the Wallarm Console user interface (UI) for configuration and security event investigation.1

Type of Federal Data:
The system is categorized at the Moderate security impact level. The federal data and information stored, processed, or transmitted includes:
- Full HTTP/HTTPS request and response bodies and headers for WAF analysis, which may contain customer API data.
- Security events, WAF rules, user profile and session data, and audit logs.
The system does not process, store, or transmit information requiring special protective measures beyond the standard FedRAMP Moderate baseline, such as Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI).

NinjaOne for Government delivers a cloud-native unified IT operations platform built for endpoint management, automation, and security at scale. IT teams can automate patching, enforce policies, and manage all endpoints from a single, unified interface. With industry-leading time to value, agencies can be fully implemented in under 30 days and achieve operator proficiency in under 10. NinjaOne requires no infrastructure overhead, allowing agencies to modernize IT operations and shift resources from reactive tasks to mission-critical objectives. 

 
Key Components of the NinjaOne for Government platform include: 

- Endpoint management 

- Windows patching 

- Patching catalog 

- SSO 

- Remote access 

- Reporting

products.csp: Covergent Solutions Inc dba Exiger Government Solutions (EGS)
products.description: Exiger Government Solutions (EGS) developed a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) product named 1Exiger which provides access to multiple modules - Due Diligence Intelligence (DDIQ), Third Party Management (3PM), Supply Dynamics (SDX), and Analytics.

Veridix? Advantage eClinical? is an integrated collection of web-enabled application subsystems and utilities specifically designed, configured, and deployed to ensure secure data entry and to satisfy the reporting needs of the clinical trials and research studies.

GetEkko is delivered as a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) offering using a multi-tenant public cloud computing provider that allows GetEkko to create dedicated customer environments for data isolation and segmentation. It is available to customers that require identity-proofing in conjunction with secure transmission of messages without the ability to read the payload in transit to users within groups, based upon schedules, or availability within the central platform.

Verily Pre is an AI-native data platform purpose-built for precision health. Pre enables federal agencies, academic institutions, and commercial organizations to harmonize, govern, discover, explore, and analyze complex multimodal biomedical data ? and to build and deploy AI models and agents directly into care and research workflows.

The platform includes  three integrated capabilities:
-  Refinery, a FHIR-native data curation engine that ingests and enriches siloed clinical and biomedical data into research- and AI-ready assets
- Exchange, a secure marketplace for discovering and sharing AI-ready datasets, models, and agents
- Workbench, a collaborative research environment with advanced cohort building, visualization, interactive analysis, batch workflow, and governance tooling.

The platform is built on infrastructure from multiple FedRAMP-authorized cloud providers and supports HIPAA compliance, role-based access controls, audit logging, and data use agreement enforcement.

Kovr AI is a patented AI-native cyber compliance automation platform purpose-built for organizations navigating the most demanding federal and commercial cybersecurity frameworks including FedRAMP, Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC 2.0), NIST SP 800-53, NIST SP 800-171, DoD Security Requirements Guide (SRG), FISMA, DoD Risk Management Framework (RMF), PCI-DSS, ISO 27001, and more. Designed to replace the manual, document-heavy workflows that have historically made federal authorization a multi-year, multi-million-dollar undertaking, Kovr compresses the path from readiness to Authorization to Operate (ATO) by automating the most labor-intensive phases of the compliance lifecycle.
 
Kovr's AI-powered engine empowers users with evidence processing, control analysis, and artifact generation including System Security Plans (SSPs), Security Assessment Reports (SARs), Plans of Action and Milestones (POA&Ms), and Security Requirements Traceability Matrices (SRTMs) directly from system documentation inputs reducing artifact preparation time from weeks to days. The platform natively supports NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 and 800-171 control baselines, OSCAL-format exports, and continuous controls monitoring (CCM), enabling organizations to maintain their authorization posture between assessment cycles and satisfy evolving continuous monitoring requirements across FedRAMP Rev 5, CMMC, and DoD SRG frameworks.
 
Artemis, Kovr's secure AI enclave, allows ISSOs, compliance officers, and security teams to generate control implementation narratives, draft assessment responses, and accelerate evidence collection without exposing sensitive system data to external AI services. Pre-built connectors and automated evidence ingestion eliminate manual artifact management, while integrated POA&M lifecycle tracking provides real-time visibility into remediation status.
